examples: This is my new pencil case.
These are your new tables and chairs.
These are new pencils and pens.
Those are your new coat hooks.
There is a new pencil case.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Do you like our new [noun]? Yes, we do.
examples: Do you like our new things? Yes, we do.
Do you like our new board? Yes, we do.
Do you like our new computer? Yes, I do.

grammar_patterns.pattern: There are lots of [plural noun] on the [noun].
examples: There are lots of pictures and posters on the wall.
There are lots of books on the table.
There are lots of posters on the board.

grammar_patterns.pattern: There are [number] [plural noun] in my class.
examples: There are 12 girls and 14 boys in my class.
There are 12 girls in my class.
There are 14 boys in my class.

phonics.sound: the alphabet and initial letter sounds
chant: A, B, C, D, E, F, G,
Come and play this game with me.
H, I, J, K, L, M, N,
Close your eyes and count to ten.
O, P, Q, R, S, T,
Touch your nose and touch your knee.
U, V, W, X, Y, Z,
Touch your feet and jump with me.

key_sentences: Look! These are your new tables and chairs.
Those are your new coat hooks.
Do you like our new things? Yes, we do.
This is my new pencil case.
Our classroom is upstairs. It's very big.
There are 12 girls and 14 boys in my class.
There are lots of pictures and posters on the wall.
